Excellence Through Inclusion
ICS advances inclusion and representation in computing, empowering individuals of all identities and backgrounds.
ICS Summer Academy
Led by distinguished UCI Faculty and amazing UCI undergrad and grad students, high school students explore new cutting edge areas of ICS, including DATA analysis and AI/ML.
Learn more about ICSSA
Tutoring Program
OAI offers tutoring on general concepts, practice problems, and theories for a variety of ICS intro courses for all students. Book an appointment with a learning mentor today!
Learn more about OAI tutoring
Programs & Resources
OAI offers a suite of programs and resources for students, faculty, and staff within ICS. We encourage you to explore our services, and would deeply appreciate your feedback.

Grand Opening of the Outreach, Access & Inclusion Student Hub
January 15, 2025
One of the most diverse and intellectually rich schools of computing in the nation, ICS is a vibrant learning community with more than 3,600 undergraduate students, 400 doctoral students and postdocs, 600 research and professional master’s students, and 100 faculty representing 3 departments (Computer Science, Informatics, Statistics), many of whom hold joint appointments at schools across campus, including business, education, engineering, medicine, natural sciences, and social sciences. The ICS community includes a network of more than 15,000 alums spread across the United States and around the globe.
Marios Papaefthymiou, ICS Dean

The OAI Tutoring⁺ Program is designed to foster academic success and inclusivity. All ICS students have access to personalized, high-quality tutoring, focusing on a broad range of subjects in lower-division ICS courses. In addition to academic support, students gain access to peer-to-peer mentoring and the OAI community. This program ensures that every student has the opportunity and support to succeed in their academic endeavors.
Why utitilize this student resource?
Personalized Learning:
Get one-on-one tutoring tailored to your unique learning style and needs.
Learning Mentors:
Learn from peers who have excelled in the same courses and are trained to help you understand complex concepts.
Flexible Scheduling:
Tutoring
appointments
are available in-person (Monday & Tuesday) and online (Wednesday & Thursday).
Drop-in
hours are available in-person (Monday & Tuesday) and virtually (Wednesday & Thursday)
Free of Charge:
This program is offered at no cost to all ICS students.
Tutoring is available for the following lower-division ICS courses:
ICS 6B
– Boolean Math
ICS 6D
– Discrete Math
ICS 31
– Introduction to Programming
ICS 32
– Programming with Software Libraries
ICS 33
– Intermediate Programming
ICS 45C
– Programming in C/C++
ICS 46
– Data Structure
ICS 51
– Introduction to Computer Organization
ICS 53
– Principles in System Design
Please email us at
oai@ics.uci.edu
if you need help scheduling an appointment or have questions about the OAI Tutoring⁺ Program.

Vision
We are committed to diversifying the tech sector and making all areas of computing inclusive across the intersections of gender identification, race, ethnicity, socioeconomic status, sexual orientation, and disability status.
Mission
We strive to build a dynamic, inclusive community of students, educators, and researchers focused on amplifying the participation and success of individuals from historically marginalized groups in computing.
Goals
Support, educate and mentor students from populations underrepresented in computing.
Foster an inclusive space for meaningful academic and professional engagement among all stakeholders in computing, regardless of identity.
Cultivate advocacy and community-building skills through targeted training.

ICS Summer Academy
The ICS Summer Academy (ICSSA) provides local high school students with opportunities to explore new cutting edge areas of ICS while immersing themselves in a collegiate environment. Students receive instructions and mentorship from ICS faculty, undergrad and graduate students to tackle real-world issues in the areas of AI/ML, data science, cryptography and software engineering.
Learn More
Accessing STEM in Higher Education
Accessing STEM in Higher Education
is a half-day annual conference at UC Irvine, to empower disabled high school students in Orange County by providing knowledge about college disability services, exposure to assistive technologies, connections with inspiring role models and mentors, and collaborative support networks, all aimed at fostering success in STEM careers.
Learn More
Aspirations in Computing
In collaboration with the
National Center for Women in Information Technology (NCWIT)
, the Aspirations in Computing (AiC) program honors 9th-12th grade students who identify as women, genderqueer, or non-binary for their computing or tech related achievements and interests. AiC encourages students to pursue their passions and connects them to resources and a community of like-minded peers. Our AiC recipients are selected based on their aptitude and aspirations in technology and computing, as demonstrated by their computing experience, computing-related activities, leadership experience, and persistence in the face of barriers to access.
Learn More

OAI Workshops
The OAI Workshops are designed to provide incoming freshmen and transfer ICS students with the foundational skills crucial for academic success. Each quarter focuses on a key area: Debugging Techniques (Fall), Introduction to GIT (Winter), and Code Review Best Practices (Spring). These workshops enhance the educational journey for new students, ensuring they are well-prepared to tackle the challenges and opportunities in their academic careers.
Computing Conferences
The OAI Conferences Scholarship enables undergraduate and graduate students to attend leading computing conferences, including the Grace Hopper Celebration, Tapia Conference, and Great Minds in STEM. These events provide access to top companies, insights into graduate programs, and opportunities to build professional networks. They serve as a bridge between academic studies and real-world careers in industry and academia.
Learn More
OAI Tutoring
The OAI Tutoring Program is designed to foster academic success and inclusivity. All ICS students have access to personalized, high-quality tutoring, focusing on a broad range of subjects in ICS lower-division courses.  This program ensures that every student has the opportunity to thrive and succeed in their academic endeavors.
Learn More
